name: AH_Bot
//: Per-stage model overrides. Omit any field to fall back to 'model' above.
Available OpenAI models used here (relative rankings are approximate):
Reasoning power:  gpt-5 > o3 > gpt-4o > o3-mini > gpt-5.4-mini > gpt-4o-mini > gpt-5.4-nano
Latency (fastest first): gpt-5.4-nano > gpt-4o-mini > gpt-5.4-mini > gpt-4o > o3-mini > gpt-5 > o3
Cost (cheapest first):   gpt-5.4-nano < gpt-4o-mini < gpt-5.4-mini < gpt-4o < o3-mini < gpt-5 < o3
gpt-5         — highest overall capability of this set; slower and more expensive; best for hardest planning/spec tasks
gpt-5.4-mini  — stronger mini reasoning model; good balance of quality, latency, and cost
gpt-5.4-nano  — cheapest / lightest of this set; best for simple high-volume deterministic mediation
gpt-4o        — strong general-purpose model; faster than flagship reasoning models; still available in API
gpt-4o-mini   — lightweight, fast, and inexpensive; good for structured dispatch / selection
o3            — strongest dedicated reasoning model in this list after gpt-5; slowest and most expensive
o3-mini       — smaller reasoning model; better latency/cost than o3 with weaker reasoning depth
- Rankings are practical engineering guidance, not exact published benchmarks.
- ChatGPT browser labels such as Instant / Thinking / Pro are product labels, not API model ids.
- GPT-4o was retired in ChatGPT but remains available in the API.
